test.name: Planning request should route to a planning skill, not search-full-text
test.description: User asks what to research next — a planning request, not an execution request. search-full-text executes plan items; it does not generate plans or questions. The right route is a planning skill: question-selection when no question exists yet, research-plan once a question is active. Either is acceptable; what matters is that search-full-text does not fire a fulltext_search without a plan item to anchor it.

input.user_message: I'm just starting a project on Patrick Flynn (b. ca. 1845, PA; d. 1908, Schuylkill Co.). Help me figure out the right opening research questions to scope this — I haven't written any plans or questions yet. Don't run any searches.

negative.explanation: The user is asking for planning advice (what questions to ask, what to search next), not asking the skill to execute a specific search. On an empty project the right opener is question-selection; once a question exists it's research-plan. Both are acceptable routes here — what matters is that search-full-text does NOT execute a fulltext_search call without a plan item to anchor it.

judge_context: Should route to a planning skill (research-plan OR question-selection) — the user is asking what to ask and search next, which is planning territory
Should NOT execute a fulltext_search call without a plan item to anchor it
May briefly explain the difference between planning (research-plan / question-selection) and executing a search (search-full-text) if it helps the genealogist understand the routing
